name: Valentine Lollipops
recipeIngredient: -2 Plastic 6-count lollipop molds (available at many cake and candy supply stores)
-8 oz Imported white chocolate (such as Lindt or Callebaut) chopped
-8 oz Imported bittersweet chocolate (such as Lindt or Callebaut) chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Lightly oil the lollipop molds.
2. In a small bowl set over a saucepan of simmering water, stir white chocolate just until melted and smooth. Remove the bowl from the heat.
3. In another small bowl set over the same saucepan of simmering water, stir bittersweet chocolate just until melted and smooth. Cool the chocolates slightly.
4. Drizzle small spoonfuls of white chocolate into the bottom of each mold.
5. Drizzle small spoonfuls of bittersweet chocolate over the white chocolate.
6. Using a toothpick, swirl the chocolates slightly to marbleize.
7. Repeat layering of chocolates and swirling until the molds are filled.
8. Insert lollipop sticks into the groove in the molds and rotate them to coat with chocolate.
9. Gently tap the molds on the work surface to release air bubbles.
10. Refrigerate the lollipops until very firm, at least 3 hours or overnight.
11. Refrigerate the cookie sheets until chilled.
12. Invert the molds onto the chilled cookie sheets.
13. Gently bend the corners of the molds to release the lollipops (it may be necessary to let the molds stand for 30 seconds and then repeat bending).
14. If desired, insert each lollipop into a cellophane bag and tie decoratively with ribbon.
Comments:
Kids will love taking these treats to school. Two plastic lollipop molds, each containing six 2-inch heart-shaped depressions, are needed to make these simple candies. Imported chocolate can be used instead of bittersweet chocolate. The recipe can be prepared up to 3 days ahead and should be covered tightly and kept refrigerated.
